## reference · monorepo single source of truth shared code governance
* A monorepo is a single repository containing multiple distinct projects, with well-defined relationships.
* A good monorepo is the opposite of monolithic.
* Polyrepo (multiple repositories) creates friction, including cumbersome code sharing, significant code duplication, costly cross-repo changes, and hard to enforce conventions.
* Monorepo benefits include share code without publishing overhead, single source of truth, atomic commits across projects, and enforceable conventions at scale.
* Monorepos amplify AI agents by providing full visibility, context flows freely, cross-cutting work, and instant feedback loops.
* Synthetic monorepos connect separate repositories into a unified dependency graph without moving any code.
* A best-in-class monorepo tool provides local caching, remote caching, distributed task execution, affected detection, task splitting, and deflaking.
* Nx is an example of a monorepo tool that checks all these boxes.
* A monorepo removes the AI ceiling by providing structural advantage for AI agents to access context and run cross-project tasks.
* Resources for learning more about monorepos include videos, podcasts, and articles on monorepo.tools and nx.dev.
Sources: https://monorepo.tools/
